---
title: "Pipeline"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

The measurement is a [Kedro](https://kedro.org) project: pure algorithms in `transfer_risk.lib`, thin nodes that read and write through a typed Data Catalog, and pipelines wired one stage at a time (data, models, similarity, attacks, transfer, risk, reporting). The graph below is the live Kedro-Viz export — pan, zoom, and click a node or dataset to see its inputs, outputs, and layer.

This is the full production pipeline — ten surrogates and five attack recipes (the 50 `(surrogate, recipe)` attack cells), wired `data → models → similarity → attacks → transfer → risk → reporting`. For readability the attack stage is drawn here as one node per cell; the actual run splits each cell into several example-shards scaled to the box's cores, so a slow cell spreads across cores, then a reduce step concatenates them — `ParallelRunner` runs the shards and `kedro run --only-missing-outputs` resumes. Click a node or dataset to see its inputs, outputs, and layer; use the focus and pipeline filters to isolate a stage.

```{=html}
<iframe class="viz-frame" src="pipeline-viz/index.html" title="Kedro pipeline graph"></iframe>
```
